comment: Fun fact : when I ate normally and frequently training gave me a headache every time . Thirty years later I fast 17-18 hours and then do battle rope and heavy lifting ( heavy for me) and no headache never take a pill anymore - I must be getting old !!!!!!!! Oh and I lift heavier than back in the day . Hmmmm . In fact the only pill I take these days is the red-pill .

I grew up with my grandparents.  What they eat every day would be thought of today as super unhealthy by the "experts" today.  They eat a lot of eggs, meat, and fat.   My grandma would make a pie every other day for decades, and we eat pie every night.   My grandma added lard  and a lot of whole eggs to the crust of pies, she even added lard to pasta to keep it from boiling over.  If my granda made something you could be that she would put lard in it if she could.   They both had "high" choleste

"...the cell isnt going to use both [fat and glucose] if the cell has access if there's more fats available to the cell than glucose no surpise it shifts its metabolic function to rely on fats as a primary fuel source, if there's more glucose available than fats no surprise the cell will shift its metabolic processes to rely more heavily on the glucose as the primary fuel." I wonder if this inefficiency in energy production means that more heat is generated? Living in coldish country and working in cold envinronment, it's kind of great if body generates more heat. Mitochodrial uncoupling sounds like it as this "uncoupling" is often mentioned in brown fat stuff.. In these things losing bodyweight kind of seems like to be the main topic and that's just irrelevant for me, no problem staying light weight, but increasing heat production would be just awesome.

Food processing companies, like Pillsbury, want to sell and/or use high gluten wheat flour (durum or spring wheat).  My Dad was an engineer for Pillsbury.   He worked on a research project to extract gluten from some wheat flour in order to enhance the percentage of gluten in other wheat flour.   Gluten is the structure that allows the bread rise more - more holes.  Some people like that type of bread.  Try eating low rise bread, like naan.  Maybe it is using a wheat plant that produces less glu
